behavior_summary: This script implements the 'U8P_LEVER::look' description and the 'U8P_LEVER::use' interaction for a lever object (U8P_LEVER).
U8P_LEVER::look: when the player inspects the lever it reads the lever frame (Item::getFrame(this)) and emits a short bark: 'broken lever' if the frame is 0x01 or 0x04, otherwise 'lever'.

U8P_LEVER::use: when the player activates the lever it normalizes the lever frame and plays SFX, then branches on the lever's quality (Item::getQuality(this)) to perform one of several gameplay effects. Quality-driven behaviors include delegating U8P_LEVER::use to another item, spawning positional effects, toggling camera inversion, activating/deactivating sliders, accumulating intelligence/stat, performing a randomized assignment/shuffle across a set of targets, and marking that a platform should appear. Many branches spawn other processes or items and call auxiliary systems (spawn, Item::use, TonysBalls, SLIDER methods).
U8P_LEVER::func0B34: small helper invoked by U8P_LEVER::use to set frames and play a sound while launching a background/free-process; appears to coordinate a short visual/audio update sequence.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LEVER::look: single conditional branch based on Item::getFrame(this) == 0x01 || 0x04 -> bark 'broken lever' else bark 'lever' (high confidence).
U8P_LEVER::use: reads current frame and, if broken-like frames detected, plays an SFX and sets the frame to a normalized broken frame (0x01 or 0x04), then always plays another SFX and continues.

U8P_LEVER::use: dispatches behavior by Item::getQuality(this) (observed branches for quality values 0x01..0x07). Each quality branch triggers distinct side-effects (internal spawns, Item::use calls, TonysBalls spawns, Camera::invertScreen toggles, SLIDER activation/deactivation, a randomization/shuffle routine, or setting makePlatformAppear). Confidence per-branch varies (medium) because exact high-level intent is inferred from call patterns.

U8P_LEVER::func0B34: iterates over a small frame/count range (appears 0..3), calls Item::setFrame and playSFX for the target, spawns FREE::func1C19 and suspends while coordinating with that spawned process (medium confidence).
open_questions: What exact gameplay effect does TonysBalls produce when invoked from the quality==0x02 branch (inferred to be a positional effect/spawn but not certain)? (low confidence)
How does the global flag makePlatformAppear interact with the rest of the game world (when and which system consumes it to actually create the platform)? (medium confidence)

Precise semantics of several low-level 'unknown' operations and some status bit manipulations (Item::orStatus / Item::pop) used in spawn branches—these are inferred from call patterns but not confirmed. (low confidence)

Exact parameter meanings for some playSFX invocations and the relationship between frame ids used in U8P_LEVER::func0B34 and the visual state targeted—frame mapping is inferred but not certain. (low confidence)

main_function.name: U8P_LEVER::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short, player-facing description of the lever's state when inspected (reports 'broken lever' for specific broken frames else 'lever').
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' or examines the lever object.
Reads Item::getFrame(this) to decide which short bark to emit.

function_map.name: U8P_LEVER::look
function_map.role: inspect/description handler (emits short bark based on frame)

function_map.name: U8P_LEVER::use
function_map.role: primary activation handler; updates lever frame/SFX and dispatches quality-dependent gameplay effects (spawns, toggles, slider ops, randomization, platform flag)

function_map.name: U8P_LEVER::func0B34
function_map.role: visual/audio update helper; sets frames, plays SFX, spawns a background free-process and coordinates with it

internal_calls.purpose: Perform a visual/audio update on the lever or a related item after activation (set frames + play SFX + spawn a helper process).

external_calls.purpose: Read lever's visual frame to decide whether to show 'broken lever' vs 'lever'.
Inspect current frame to determine normalization and play initial SFX for broken-like frames.
Set the lever's visual frame after activation (normalize to broken frame or alternate frame).
Provide audio feedback for activation and per-quality effects.
Select the behavior branch based on the lever's configured quality/mode value.
Delegate activation to another item in at least one quality branch (quality == 0x01 inferred).
Spawn or trigger a positional effect/entity using coordinates from an item reference (quality == 0x02 inferred).
Toggle/invert screen visual effect for certain quality branches (visual feedback).
Activate or deactivate slider objects spawned/referenced by the lever (quality == 0x05 inferred).
Increment a stat/score as a side-effect in at least one branch (inferred).
Generate random indices used to shuffle or randomly assign items/options in the quality == 0x06 branch.
Assign quality/mode values to items created/updated by lever branches (inferred from patterns).
Instantiate items/NPCs and set status/targets during spawning or when 'making a platform appear' (quality == 0x07 inferred).
Launch a background/free-process as part of the visual/audio update helper and suspend while coordinating with it.

writes.name: makePlatformAppear
writes.likely_effect: Set to true when quality == 0x07 branch runs, marking that a platform (and associated spawn/NPC behavior) should be created or activated elsewhere in the world.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the 'U8P_LEVER::look' description and the 'U8P_LEVER::use' interaction for a lever object (U8P_LEVER).
